|-
| rowspan="2" | 231
| rowspan="2" | {{a|Shadow Shield}}
| class="l" | Reduces damage taken when HP is full.
{{Gentable|VII|rowspan=2}}
|-
| class="l" | Halves the damage the Pokémon takes while its HP is full.
|-
| rowspan="2" | 232
| rowspan="2" | {{a|Prism Armor}}
| class="l" | Reduces the power of supereffective attacks that hit the Pokémon.
{{Gentable|VII|rowspan=2}}
|-
| class="l" | Reduces the damage the Pokémon takes from supereffective moves by 25%.
|-
| rowspan="2" | 233
| rowspan="2" | {{a|Neuroforce}}
| class="l" | Powers up the Pokémon's supereffective attacks even further.
{{Gentable|VII|rowspan=2}}
|-
| class="l" | Boosts the power of the Pokémon's supereffective attacks by 25%.
|-
| rowspan="2" | 234
| rowspan="2" | {{a|Intrepid Sword}}
| class="l" | Boosts the Pokémon's Attack on first entry.
{{Gentable|VIII|rowspan=2}}
|-
| class="l" | Boosts the Pokémon's Attack stat by 1 stage the first time the Pokémon enters a battle.
|-
| rowspan="2" | 235
| rowspan="2" | {{a|Dauntless Shield}}
| class="l" | Boosts the Pokémon's Defense on first entry.
{{Gentable|VIII|rowspan=2}}
|-
| class="l" | Boosts the Pokémon's Defense stat by 1 stage the first time the Pokémon enters a battle.
|-
| rowspan="2" | 236
| rowspan="2" | {{a|Libero}}
| class="l" | Changes the Pokémon's type to the type of the move it's about to use.
{{Gentable|VIII|rowspan=2}}
|-
| class="l" | Changes the Pokémon's type to the type of the move it's about to use. This works only once per time the Pokémon enters battle.
|-
| rowspan="2" | 237
| rowspan="2" | {{a|Ball Fetch}}
| class="l" | If not holding an item, retrieves the first failed Poké Ball.
{{Gentable|VIII|rowspan=2}}
|-
| class="l" | If the Pokémon is not holding an item, it will fetch the Poké Ball from the first failed throw of the battle.
|-
| rowspan="2" | 238
| rowspan="2" | {{a|Cotton Down}}
| class="l" | Lowers all other Pokémon’s Speed when hit.
{{Gentable|VIII|rowspan=2}}
|-
| class="l" | When this Pokémon is hit by an attack, it scatters cotton fluff around, lowering the Speed of all other Pokémon by 1 stage.
|-
| rowspan="2" | 239
| rowspan="2" | {{a|Propeller Tail}}
| class="l" | Ignores the effects of Abilities and moves that draw in moves.
{{Gentable|VIII|rowspan=2}}
|-
| class="l" | The Pokémon ignores the effects of Abilities and moves that draw in moves.
|-
| rowspan="2" | 240
| rowspan="2" | {{a|Mirror Armor}}
| class="l" | Bounces back only the stat-lowering effects that the Pokémon receives.
{{Gentable|VIII|rowspan=2}}
|-
| class="l" | Instead of being affected by stat-lowering effects, the Pokémon bounces them back at whichever Pokémon caused them.
|-
| rowspan="2" | 241
| rowspan="2" | {{a|Gulp Missile}}
| class="l" | After using Surf or Dive, returns with prey and when hit, spits it out to damage and hinder the attacker.
{{Gentable|VIII|rowspan=2}}
|-
| class="l" | After using Surf or Dive, the Pokémon returns with prey, changing form based on its HP. Above half HP, it catches an Arrokuda. At half HP or less, a Pikachu. When it takes damage, it spits the prey at the attacker, dealing 1/4 of the attacker’s max HP. Arrokuda lowers the attacker’s Defense by 1 stage, Pikachu paralyzes it.
|-
| rowspan="2" | 242
| rowspan="2" | {{a|Stalwart}}
| class="l" | Ignores the effects of Abilities and moves that draw in moves.
{{Gentable|VIII|rowspan=2}}
|-
| class="l" | The Pokémon ignores the effects of Abilities and moves that draw in moves.
|-
| rowspan="2" | 243
| rowspan="2" | {{a|Steam Engine}}
| class="l" | Boosts the Pokémon's Speed stat drastically if hit by a Fire- or Water-type move.
{{Gentable|VIII|rowspan=2}}
|-
| class="l" | Boosts the Pokémon's Speed stat to its sixth stage when the Pokémon is hit by a Fire- or Water-type move. If the Pokémon is in the first party slot, Pokémon eggs hatch twice as fast.
|-
| rowspan="2" | 244
| rowspan="2" | {{a|Punk Rock}}
| class="l" | Boosts sound-based moves and reduces their damage taken.
{{Gentable|VIII|rowspan=2}}
|-
| class="l" | Boosts the power of sound-based moves by 30%. The Pokémon also takes half damage from these kinds of moves.
|-
| rowspan="2" | 245
| rowspan="2" | {{a|Sand Spit}}
| class="l" | Summons a sandstorm when the Pokémon is hit by an attack.
{{Gentable|VIII|rowspan=2}}
|-
| class="l" | Summons a sandstorm for 5 turns when the Pokémon takes damage from moves.
|-
| rowspan="2" | 246
| rowspan="2" | {{a|Ice Scales}}
| class="l" | Halves damage taken from special moves.
{{Gentable|VIII|rowspan=2}}
|-
| class="l" | The Pokémon is protected by ice scales, which halve the damage taken from special moves.
|-
| rowspan="2" | 247
| rowspan="2" | {{a|Ripen}}
| class="l" | Ripens Berries and doubles their effect.
{{Gentable|VIII|rowspan=2}}
|-
| class="l" | Doubles the effects of Berries eaten by the Pokémon.
|-
| rowspan="2" | 248
| rowspan="2" | {{a|Ice Face}}
| class="l" | Blocks the first physical hit and changes form, then restores in snow.
{{Gentable|VIII|rowspan=2}}
|-
| class="l" | When the Pokémon is in its Ice Face form and is hit by a physical move, it takes no damage and changes into its Noice Face Form. Its Ice Face is restored when snow begins or if it enters battle during snow.
|-
| rowspan="2" | 249
| rowspan="2" | {{a|Power Spot}}
| class="l" | Just being next to the Pokémon powers up moves.
{{Gentable|VIII|rowspan=2}}
|-
| class="l" | Boosts the power of moves used by the Pokémon's allies by 30%.
|-
| rowspan="2" | 250
| rowspan="2" | {{a|Mimicry}}
| class="l" | Changes the Pokémon's type based on the terrain.
{{Gentable|VIII|rowspan=2}}
|-
| class="l" | The Pokémon's type changes to match the terrain.
|-
| rowspan="2" | 251
| rowspan="2" | {{a|Screen Cleaner}}
| class="l" | When the Pokémon enters a battle, it removes Light Screen, Reflect, and Aurora Veil.
{{Gentable|VIII|rowspan=2}}
|-
| class="l" | When the Pokémon enters a battle, the effects of Light Screen, Reflect, and Aurora Veil are nullified for both opposing and ally Pokémon.
|-
| rowspan="2" | 252
| rowspan="2" | {{a|Steely Spirit}}
| class="l" | Boosts Steel-type moves for itself and its allies.
{{Gentable|VIII|rowspan=2}}
|-
| class="l" | The Pokémon’s ironclad spirit empowers it and its allies, boosting the power of their Steel-type moves by 50%. This effect stacks if multiple Pokémon with this Ability are on the field.
|-
| rowspan="2" | 253
| rowspan="2" | {{a|Perish Body}}
| class="l" | When hit by a contact move, the Pokémon and the attacker will faint after three turns.
{{Gentable|VIII|rowspan=2}}
|-
| class="l" | When hit by a move that makes direct contact, the Pokémon and the attacker will faint after three turns unless they switch out of battle.
|-
| rowspan="2" | 254
| rowspan="2" | {{a|Wandering Spirit}}
| class="l" | Swaps abilities with a Pokémon that makes contact.
{{Gentable|VIII|rowspan=2}}
|-
| class="l" | When the Pokémon is hit by a contact move, it swaps Abilities with the attacker.
|-
| rowspan="2" | 255
| rowspan="2" | {{a|Gorilla Tactics}}
| class="l" | Boosts the Pokémon's Attack stat but only allows the use of a single move.
{{Gentable|VIII|rowspan=2}}
|-
| class="l" | Boosts the holder's Attack stat by 50%. However, once the Pokémon uses a move, it becomes unable to use any other move but that one until it switches out of battle.
|-
| rowspan="2" | 256
| rowspan="2" | {{a|Neutralizing Gas}}
| class="l" | Nullifies the Abilities of other Pokémon.
{{Gentable|VIII|rowspan=2}}
|-
| class="l" | While the Pokémon is in the battle, the effects of all other Pokémon's Abilities will be nullified or will not be triggered.
|-
| rowspan="2" | 257
| rowspan="2" | {{a|Pastel Veil}}
| class="l" | Prevents the Pokémon and its allies from being poisoned.
{{Gentable|VIII|rowspan=2}}
|-
| class="l" | Prevents the Pokémon and its allies from being poisoned or badly poisoned.
|-
| rowspan="2" | 258
| rowspan="2" | {{a|Hunger Switch}}
| class="l" | The Pokémon changes its form after every turn.
{{Gentable|VIII|rowspan=2}}
|-
| class="l" | The Pokémon changes its form, alternating between its Full Belly Mode and Hangry Mode at the end of every turn.
|-
| rowspan="2" | 259
| rowspan="2" | {{a|Quick Draw}}
| class="l" | Enables the Pokémon to move first occasionally.
{{Gentable|VIII|rowspan=2}}
|-
| class="l" | The Pokémon's moves have a 30% chance of going first among moves of the same priority.
|-
| rowspan="2" | 260
| rowspan="2" | {{a|Unseen Fist}}
| class="l" | If the Pokémon can make contact, it can attack a protecting target but for less damage.
{{Gentable|VIII|rowspan=2}}
|-
| class="l" | When the Pokémon uses contact moves, it can hit even targets that are protecting themselves, dealing 1/4 of the damage that the move would otherwise deal. Everything aside from the target's protective effects is still triggered.
|-
| rowspan="2" | 261
| rowspan="2" | {{a|Curious Medicine}}
| class="l" | Removes allies’ stat changes when it enters battle.
{{Gentable|VIII|rowspan=2}}
|-
| class="l" | When the Pokémon enters a battle, it scatters medicine from its shell, which removes all stat changes from allies.
|-
| rowspan="2" | 262
| rowspan="2" | {{a|Transistor}}
| class="l" | Powers up Electric-type moves.
{{Gentable|VIII|rowspan=2}}
|-
| class="l" | Boosts the power of this Pokémon’s Electric-type moves by 50%.
|-
| rowspan="2" | 263
| rowspan="2" | {{a|Dragon's Maw}}
| class="l" | Powers up Dragon-type moves.
{{Gentable|VIII|rowspan=2}}
|-
| class="l" | Boosts the power of this Pokémon’s Dragon-type moves by 50%.
|-
| rowspan="2" | 264
| rowspan="2" | {{a|Chilling Neigh}}
| class="l" | Boosts the Attack stat after knocking out any Pokémon.
{{Gentable|VIII|rowspan=2}}
|-
| class="l" | When the Pokémon knocks out a target, it utters a chilling neigh, which raises its Attack stat by 1 stage.
|-
| rowspan="2" | 265
| rowspan="2" | {{a|Grim Neigh}}
| class="l" | Boosts the Sp. Atk stat after knocking out any Pokémon.
{{Gentable|VIII|rowspan=2}}
|-
| class="l" | When the Pokémon knocks out a target, it utters a terrifying neigh, which raises its Sp. Atk stat by 1 stage.
|-
| rowspan="2" | 266
| rowspan="2" | {{a|As One}}
| class="l" | Combines the effects of Unnerve and Chilling Neigh into one Ability.
{{Gentable|VIII|rowspan=2}}
|-
| class="l" | Prevents opposing Pokémon from eating Berries. When this Pokémon knocks out a target, its Attack stat is raised by 1 stage.
|-
| rowspan="2" | 267
| rowspan="2" | {{a|As One}}
| class="l" | Combines the effects of Unnerve and Grim Neigh into one Ability.
{{Gentable|VIII|rowspan=2}}
|-
| class="l" | Prevents opposing Pokémon from eating Berries. When this Pokémon knocks out a target, its Special Attack stat is raised by 1 stage.
|-
| rowspan="2" | 268
| rowspan="2" | {{a|Lingering Aroma}}
| class="l" | Contact with the Pokémon spreads their aroma to the attacker.
{{Gentable|IX|rowspan=2}}
|-
| class="l" | Contact with the Pokémon changes the attacker's Ability to Lingering Aroma.
|-
| rowspan="2" | 269
| rowspan="2" | {{a|Seed Sower}}
| class="l" | Turns the ground into Grassy Terrain when the Pokémon is hit by an attack.
{{Gentable|IX|rowspan=2}}
|-
| class="l" | Turns the ground into Grassy Terrain for 5 turns when the Pokémon takes damage from moves.
|-
| rowspan="2" | 270
| rowspan="2" | {{a|Thermal Exchange}}
| class="l" | Boosts Attack when hit with a Fire-type move and cannot be burned.
{{Gentable|IX|rowspan=2}}
|-
| class="l" | Boosts the Attack stat by 1 stage when the Pokémon is hit by a Fire-type attack. The Pokémon also cannot be burned.
|-
| rowspan="2" | 271
| rowspan="2" | {{a|Anger Shell}}
| class="l" | Boosts Attack, Sp. Atk, and Speed but lowers defenses when HP drops below half.
{{Gentable|IX|rowspan=2}}
|-
| class="l" | When this Pokémon takes damage from a move that causes its HP to drop to half or less, it gets angry. This raises its Attack, Sp. Atk, and Speed by 1 stage, but lowers its Defense and Sp. Def by 1 stage.
|-
| rowspan="2" | 272
| rowspan="2" | {{a|Purifying Salt}}
| class="l" | Prevents status conditions and halves damage from Ghost moves.
{{Gentable|IX|rowspan=2}}
|-
| class="l" | The Pokémon's pure salt protects it from status conditions and halves the damage taken from Ghost-type moves.
|-
| rowspan="2" | 273
| rowspan="2" | {{a|Well-Baked Body}}
| class="l" | Sharply boosts its Defense stat when it's hit by an Fire-type move.
{{Gentable|IX|rowspan=2}}
|-
| class="l" | Fire-type moves do not work on the Pokémon. Instead, they boost its Defense stat by 2 stages.
|-
| rowspan="2" | 274
| rowspan="2" | {{a|Wind Rider}}
| class="l" | Raises Attack in Tailwind or when hit by a wind move.
{{Gentable|IX|rowspan=2}}
|-
| class="l" | Raises this Pokémon’s Attack by 1 stage if Tailwind is active on its side or if it is hit by a wind move. This Pokémon is also immune to damage from wind moves.
|-
| rowspan="2" | 275
| rowspan="2" | {{a|Guard Dog}}
| class="l" | Boosts Attack if intimidated. Cannot be forced to switch out.
{{Gentable|IX|rowspan=2}}
|-
| class="l" | Boosts the Pokémon's Attack stat by 1 stage instead of lowering it if intimidated. Moves and items that would force the Pokémon to switch out of battle also fail to work.
|-
| rowspan="2" | 276
| rowspan="2" | {{a|Rocky Payload}}
| class="l" | Powers up Rock-type moves.
{{Gentable|IX|rowspan=2}}
|-
| class="l" | Boosts the power of this Pokémon’s Rock-type moves by 50%.
|-
| rowspan="2" | 277
| rowspan="2" | {{a|Wind Power}}
| class="l" | Becomes charged in Tailwind or when hit by a wind move, doubling its next Electric move.
{{Gentable|IX|rowspan=2}}
|-
| class="l" | If Tailwind is active on its side or it is hit by a wind move, the Pokémon becomes charged, doubling the power of the next Electric-type move it uses.
|-
| rowspan="2" | 278
| rowspan="2" | {{a|Zero to Hero}}
| class="l" | The Pokémon transforms into its Hero Form when it switches out.
{{Gentable|IX|rowspan=2}}
|-
| class="l" | The Pokémon changes into its Hero Form when it switches out of battle.
|-
| rowspan="2" | 279
| rowspan="2" | {{a|Commander}}
| class="l" | Enters an ally Dondozo’s mouth, becoming untargetable and boosting Dondozo's stats.
{{Gentable|IX|rowspan=2}}
|-
| class="l" | When this Pokémon enters battle with Dondozo, it goes inside its mouth, becoming unable to act or be targeted. While inside, it raises Dondozo’s Attack, Defense, Sp. Atk, Sp. Def, and Speed by 2 stages each. If the Pokémon is in the first party slot, wild encounters have a 30% chance to be double battles.
|-
| rowspan="2" | 280
| rowspan="2" | {{a|Electromorphosis}}
| class="l" | Becomes charged in when hit, doubling its next Electric move.
{{Gentable|IX|rowspan=2}}
|-
| class="l" | The Pokémon becomes charged when it takes damage, doubling the power of the next Electric-type move the Pokémon uses.
|-
| rowspan="2" | 281
| rowspan="2" | {{a|Protosynthesis}}
| class="l" | Boosts the Pokémon's highest stat in harsh sunlight or if the it's holding Booster Energy.
{{Gentable|IX|rowspan=2}}
|-
| class="l" | Boosts the Pokémon's most proficient stat by 30%, or 50% if Speed is the most proficient, in harsh sunlight or if the Pokémon is holding Booster Energy.
|-
| rowspan="2" | 282
| rowspan="2" | {{a|Quark Drive}}
| class="l" | Boosts the Pokémon's highest stat in Electric Terrain or if the it's holding Booster Energy.
{{Gentable|IX|rowspan=2}}
|-
| class="l" | Boosts the Pokémon's most proficient stat by 30%, or 50% if Speed is the most proficient, in on Electric Terrain or if the Pokémon is holding Booster Energy.
|-
| rowspan="2" | 283
| rowspan="2" | {{a|Good as Gold}}
| class="l" | The Pokémon is immune to status moves.
{{Gentable|IX|rowspan=2}}
|-
| class="l" | A body of pure, solid gold gives the Pokémon full immunity to other Pokémon's status moves.
|-
| rowspan="2" | 284
| rowspan="2" | {{a|Vessel of Ruin}}
| class="l" | Lowers Sp. Atk of all other Pokémon.
{{Gentable|IX|rowspan=2}}
|-
| class="l" | The power of the Pokémon’s ruinous vessel lowers the Sp. Atk of all Pokémon except itself by 25%.
|-
| rowspan="2" | 285
| rowspan="2" | {{a|Sword of Ruin}}
| class="l" | Lowers Defense of all other Pokémon.
{{Gentable|IX|rowspan=2}}
|-
| class="l" | The power of the Pokémon's ruinous sword lowers the Defense stats of all Pokémon except itself by 25%.
|-
| rowspan="2" | 286
| rowspan="2" | {{a|Tablets of Ruin}}
| class="l" | Lowers Attack of all other Pokémon.
{{Gentable|IX|rowspan=2}}
|-
| class="l" | The power of the Pokémon's ruinous wooden tablets lowers the Attack stats of all Pokémon except itself by 25%.
|-
| rowspan="2" | 287
| rowspan="2" | {{a|Beads of Ruin}}
| class="l" | Lowers Sp.Def of all other Pokémon.
{{Gentable|IX|rowspan=2}}
|-
| class="l" | The power of the Pokémon’s ruinous beads lowers the Sp. Def of all Pokémon excep itself by 25%.
|-
| rowspan="2" | 288
| rowspan="2" | {{a|Orichalcum Pulse}}
| class="l" | Summons harsh sunlight and boosts highest attacking stat when in harsh sunlight.
{{Gentable|IX|rowspan=2}}
|-
| class="l" | Turns the sunlight harsh for 5 turns when the Pokémon enters a battle. The ancient pulse thrumming through the Pokémon also boosts its most proficient offensive stat by 33% in harsh sunlight.
|-
| rowspan="2" | 289
| rowspan="2" | {{a|Hadron Engine}}
| class="l" | Turns ground to Electric terrain and boosts highest attacking stat when on Electric Terrain.
{{Gentable|IX|rowspan=2}}
|-
| class="l" | Turns the ground into Electric Terrain for 5 turns when the Pokémon enters a battle. The futuristic engine within the Pokémon also boosts its most proficient offensive stat by 33% on Electric Terrain.
|-
| rowspan="2" | 290
| rowspan="2" | {{a|Opportunist}}
| class="l" | Copies opponent's stat boosts whenever it can.
{{Gentable|IX|rowspan=2}}
|-
| class="l" | If an opponent's stat is boosted, the Pokémon seizes the opportunity to boost the same stat for itself.
|-
| rowspan="2" | 291
| rowspan="2" | {{a|Cud Chew}}
| class="l" | Eats a Berry again on the next turn.
{{Gentable|IX|rowspan=2}}
|-
| class="l" | When the Pokémon eats a Berry, it will regurgitate that Berry at the end of the next turn and eat it one more time.
|-
| rowspan="2" | 292
| rowspan="2" | {{a|Sharpness}}
| class="l" | Powers up slicing moves.
{{Gentable|IX|rowspan=2}}
|-
| class="l" | Boosts the power of the Pokémon's slicing moves by 50%. The Pokémon can also cut Tall Grass within a two-square radius instead of a one-square radius when using Cut outside of battle.
|-
| rowspan="2" | 293
| rowspan="2" | {{a|Supreme Overlord}}
| class="l" | Boosts damage for each fainted ally.
{{Gentable|IX|rowspan=2}}
|-
| class="l" | When the Pokémon enters a battle, the power of its moves is boosted by 10% for each Pokémon in its party that has been defeated in the battle already. The maximum boost is 50%.
|-
| rowspan="2" | 294
| rowspan="2" | {{a|Costar}}
| class="l" | When this Pokémon enters battle, it copies an ally’s stat changes.
{{Gentable|IX|rowspan=2}}
|-
| class="l" | When the Pokémon enters a battle, it copies an ally's stat changes. If the Pokémon is in the first party slot, wild encounters have a 30% chance to be double battles.
|-
| rowspan="2" | 295
| rowspan="2" | {{a|Toxic Debris}}
| class="l" | Scatters Toxic Spikes when hit by physical moves.
{{Gentable|IX|rowspan=2}}
|-
| class="l" | When this Pokémon takes damage from a physical move, it scatters a Toxic Spikes trap at the feet of the opposing team.
|-
| rowspan="2" | 296
| rowspan="2" | {{a|Armor Tail}}
| class="l" | Opponents are unable to use priority moves against the Pokémon or its allies.
{{Gentable|IX|rowspan=2}}
|-
| class="l" | The mysterious tail covering the Pokémon's head makes opponents unable to use priority moves against the Pokémon or its allies.
|-
| rowspan="2" | 297
| rowspan="2" | {{a|Earth Eater}}
| class="l" | Restores HP if hit by a Ground-type move.
{{Gentable|IX|rowspan=2}}
|-
| class="l" | Ground-type moves do not work on the Pokémon. Instead, they restore 1/4 of its max HP.
|-
| rowspan="2" | 298
| rowspan="2" | {{a|Mycelium Might}}
| class="l" | The Pokémon's status moves will happen more slowly, but ignore the target's ability.
{{Gentable|IX|rowspan=2}}
|-
| class="l" | The Pokémon will always act last within its own priority bracket when using status moves, but these moves will be unimpeded by the Ability of the target.
|-
| rowspan="2" | 299
| rowspan="2" | {{a|Hospitality}}
| class="l" | Restores ally's HP when it enters battle.
{{Gentable|IX|rowspan=2}}
|-
| class="l" | When the Pokémon enters a battle, it showers its ally with hospitality, restoring 1/4 of its max HP.
|-
| rowspan="2" | 300
| rowspan="2" | {{a|Mind's Eye}}
| class="l" | The Pokémon ignores evasiveness and accuracy changes and Ghost-type immunities.
{{Gentable|IX|rowspan=2}}
|-
| class="l" | The Pokémon ignores changes to opponents' evasiveness, its accuracy can't be lowered, and it can hit Ghost types with Normal- and Fighting-type moves.
|-
| rowspan="2" | 301
| rowspan="2" | {{a|Embody Aspect}}
| class="l" | Boosts the Pokémon's Speed stat when it Terastalizes.
{{Gentable|IX|rowspan=2}}
|-
| class="l" | The Pokémon's heart fills with memories, causing the Teal Mask to shine and the Pokémon's Speed stat to be boosted.
|-
| rowspan="2" | 302
| rowspan="2" | {{a|Embody Aspect}}
| class="l" | Boosts the Pokémon's Attack stat when it Terastalizes.
{{Gentable|IX|rowspan=2}}
|-
| class="l" | The Pokémon's heart fills with memories, causing the Hearthflame Mask to shine and the Pokémon's Attack stat to be boosted.
|-
| rowspan="2" | 303
| rowspan="2" | {{a|Embody Aspect}}
| class="l" | Boosts the Pokémon's Sp. Def stat when it Terastalizes.
{{Gentable|IX|rowspan=2}}
|-
| class="l" | The Pokémon's heart fills with memories, causing the Wellspring Mask to shine and the Pokémon's Sp. Def stat to be boosted.
|-
| rowspan="2" | 304
| rowspan="2" | {{a|Embody Aspect}}
| class="l" | Boosts the Pokémon's Defense stat when it Terastalizes.
{{Gentable|IX|rowspan=2}}
|-
| class="l" | The Pokémon's heart fills with memories, causing the Cornerstone Mask to shine and the Pokémon's Defense stat to be boosted.
|-
| rowspan="2" | 305
| rowspan="2" | {{a|Toxic Chain}}
| class="l" | May badly poison any target the Pokémon hits with a move.
{{Gentable|IX|rowspan=2}}
|-
| class="l" | The power of the Pokémon's toxic chain has a 30% chance to badly poison any target the Pokémon hits with an attack.
|-
| rowspan="2" | 306
| rowspan="2" | {{a|Supersweet Syrup}}
| class="l" | Lowers the opposing Pokémon's evasiveness stats on first entry.
{{Gentable|IX|rowspan=2}}
|-
| class="l" | A sickly sweet scent spreads across the field the first time the Pokémon enters a battle, opponents' evasiveness is lowered by 1 stage. This Ability is triggered only once per battle.
|-
| rowspan="2" | 307
| rowspan="2" | {{a|Tera Shift}}
| class="l" | The Pokémon transforms into its Terastal Form when it enters battle.
{{Gentable|IX|rowspan=2}}
|-
| class="l" | When the Pokémon enters a battle, it absorbs the energy around itself and transforms into its Terastal Form.
|-
| rowspan="2" | 308
| rowspan="2" | {{a|Tera Shell}}
| class="l" | When HP is full, all attacks are not very effective.
{{Gentable|IX|rowspan=2}}
|-
| class="l" | The Pokémon's shell contains the powers of each type. All damage-dealing moves that hit the Pokémon when its HP is full will not be very effective.
|-
| rowspan="2" | 309
| rowspan="2" | {{a|Teraform Zero}}
| class="l" | Removes weather and terrain when the Pokémon enters its Stellar Form.
{{Gentable|IX|rowspan=2}}
|-
| class="l" | When Terapagos changes into its Stellar Form, it uses its hidden powers to eliminate all effects of weather and terrain, reducing them to zero.
|-
| rowspan="2" | 310
| rowspan="2" | {{a|Poison Puppeteer}}
| class="l" | Pokemon poisoned by the Pokémon's moves will also be confused.
{{Gentable|IX|rowspan=2}}
|-
| class="l" | Pokémon poisoned or badly poisoned by Pecharunt's moves will also become confused.
|-
| rowspan="2" | 311
| rowspan="2" | {{a|Piercing Drill}}
| class="l" | If the Pokémon can make contact, it can attack a protecting target but for less damage.
{{Gentable|IX|rowspan=2}}
|-
| class="l" | When the Pokémon uses contact moves, it can hit even targets that are protecting themselves, dealing 1/4 of the damage that the move would otherwise deal. Everything aside from the target's protective effects is still triggered.
|-
| rowspan="2" | 312
| rowspan="2" | {{a|Dragonize}}
| class="l" | Normal-type moves become Dragon-type moves. The power of those moves is boosted a little.
{{Gentable|IX|rowspan=2}}
|-
| class="l" | The Pokémon's Normal-type moves become Dragon-type moves and their power is boosted by 20%.
|-
| rowspan="2" | 313
| rowspan="2" | {{a|Dragonize}}
| class="l" | Normal-type moves become Dragon-type moves. The power of those moves is boosted a little.
{{Gentable|IX|rowspan=2}}
|-
| class="l" | The Pokémon's Normal-type moves become Dragon-type moves and their power is boosted by 20%.
|-
| rowspan="2" | 314
| rowspan="2" | {{a|Dragonize}}
| class="l" | Normal-type moves become Dragon-type moves. The power of those moves is boosted a little.
{{Gentable|IX|rowspan=2}}
|-
| class="l" | The Pokémon's Normal-type moves become Dragon-type moves and their power is boosted by 20%.
|-
| rowspan="2" | 315
| rowspan="2" | {{a|Mega Sol}}
| class="l" | Moves used by the Pokémon are treated as if the sunlight is harsh.
{{Gentable|IX|rowspan=2}}
|-
| class="l" | Even when the sunlight has not turned harsh, the Pokémon can use its moves as if the weather were harsh sunlight.
|-
| rowspan="2" | 316
| rowspan="2" | {{a|Mega Sol}}
| class="l" | Moves used by the Pokémon are treated as if the sunlight is harsh.
{{Gentable|IX|rowspan=2}}
|-
| class="l" | Even when the sunlight has not turned harsh, the Pokémon can use its moves as if the weather were harsh sunlight.
|-
| rowspan="2" | 317
| rowspan="2" | {{a|Mega Sol}}
| class="l" | Moves used by the Pokémon are treated as if the sunlight is harsh.
{{Gentable|IX|rowspan=2}}
|-
| class="l" | Even when the sunlight has not turned harsh, the Pokémon can use its moves as if the weather were harsh sunlight.
|-
| rowspan="2" | 318
| rowspan="2" | {{a|Spicy Spray}}
| class="l" | Burns attackers when the Pokémon is hit by an attack.
{{Gentable|IX|rowspan=2}}
|-
| class="l" | When the Pokémon takes damage from a move, it burns the attacker.
